title: Statistical Consulting for Agricultural, Environmental, and Biological Research
text: I help research teams design experiments, analyze complex biological datasets, and communicate results clearly.

title: About
text: Celeste M. Falcon is a biostatistician with experience in agricultural research (plant breeding/quantitative genetics, biologicals discovery & development) and applied statistical consulting. She holds a PhD in Applied Plant Sciences from the University of Minnesota, Twin Cities and a BS in Plant Science from Cornell University. Previously, she was a biostatistician at Bayer Crop Science and Ginkgo Bioworks.

title: Portfolio
text:
Client projects

Larvicide efficacy analysis pipeline - Automated a scientific reporting pipeline for vector control research, reducing manual data interpretation time and ensuring statistical accuracy. Created reports with Quarto to deliver reproducible reports as production-ready PDFs. To maintain client confidentiality, this portfolio project utilizes an anonymized, synthetic dataset.
[View on GitHub] | [Sample Report (PDF)] | [Simulation Script]



Publications

Postdoctoral research: Falcon C.M., Kaeppler S.M., Spalding E.P., et al. (2020) Relative utility of agronomic, phenological, and morphological traits for assessing genotype-by-environment interaction in maize inbreds. Crop Science, 60: 62–81. [Journal Article] | [Scripts on GitHub]
Graduate research: Falcon, C.M., Horsley, R., Hu, G., Blake, T. and Smith, K.P. (2019), Mapping QTLs for Grain Protein Concentration and Agronomic Traits under Different Nitrogen Levels in Barley. Crop Science, 59: 68-83. [Journal Article]
